title: Trionic Array I
content:
You are given an integer array nums of length n.

An array is trionic if there exist indices 0 < p < q < n − 1 such that:

• nums[0...p] is strictly increasing,

• nums[p...q] is strictly decreasing,

• nums[q...n − 1] is strictly increasing.

Return true if nums is trionic, otherwise return false.

Example 1:

Input: nums = [1,3,5,4,2,6]

Output: true

Explanation: Pick p = 2, q = 4:

• nums[0...2] = [1, 3, 5] is strictly increasing (1 < 3 < 5).

• nums[2...4] = [5, 4, 2] is strictly decreasing (5 > 4 > 2).

• nums[4...5] = [2, 6] is strictly increasing (2 < 6).

Example 2:

Input: nums = [2,1,3]

Output: false

Explanation: There is no way to pick p and q to form the required three segments.

Constraints:

• 3 <= n <= 100

• -1000 <= nums[i] <= 1000

solutions.explanation: Brute-force all pairs (p,q) with 0<p<q<n-1; check three segments are strictly increasing, decreasing, increasing. O(n^4) time.
